summary: The diff addresses all three requirements: REQ-1 (fix Settings auth via useSessionAuth), REQ-2 (add 'Other' gender option), and REQ-3 (Change Password section). The backend adds #other to the Gender type and a migration module. The frontend Settings.tsx uses useSessionAuth for auth gating, includes 'Other' in gender selection, and adds a Change Password form. useSessionAuth gains a changePassword function. useAccountSettings hooks check isAuthenticated from useSessionAuth. Most ACs appear satisfied, but some details warrant scrutiny.

missing_requirements.requirement: Submitting with mismatched new/confirm passwords shows a validation error without calling the update function.
missing_requirements.reason: The Settings.tsx diff is truncated before the handleChangePassword logic is visible, so we cannot confirm the mismatch validation is fully implemented. The state variables exist but the actual guard logic is not visible in the truncated diff.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) was added to handle upgrading existing user data from the old Gender type (without #other) to the new Gender type (with #other), including Map-based migration of all userProfiles and userSettings records.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not request a migration module or any data migration strategy. It only asked for the 'Other' gender option to be added to the type and UI. This is extra infrastructure not requested.
